title: 2023 Census release schedule | Stats NZ
content:
## 2023 Census release schedule
This page provides information about the plan for releasing 2023 Census data. As we publish data, we will continually release new products that will benefit data users and their experience in working with the data from New Zealand’s latest census.
The first data and insights from the 2023 Census data were published on 29 May 2024, with Individuals and dwellings data published on 3 October 2024 and Families, extended families, and households data published on 7 November 2024. Census data and insights continued to be published through to June 2025.
2023 Census Māori descent and iwi affiliation data has also been published for iwi Māori on Te Whata for all three releases.
2023 Census Products and release finder provides detailed information about what is being released and when.
Engagement about 2023 Census data sets out opportunities to learn more about 2023 Census products and services.
Te Kāhui Raraunga via Te Whata
29 May 2024 – First data release (Published) Census usually resident population counts by ethnic group and age (Excel, web map) Dwelling counts (Excel) Māori descent population counts by age (Excel, web map) Metadata 2023 Census product and release finder
29 May 2024 – First data release (Published)
- Census usually resident population counts by ethnic group and age (Excel, web map)
- Dwelling counts (Excel)
- Māori descent population counts by age (Excel, web map)
- 2023 Census product and release finder
29 May 2024 – First data release (Published) Māori descent population counts by age Census usually resident population counts by ethnic group and age
29 May 2024 – First data release (Published)
- Māori descent population counts by age
- Census usually resident population counts by ethnic group and age
26 September 2024 – Te Ara Takatū (Published) Customised iwi data requests on individuals and dwellings data Metadata

3 October 2024 – Individuals and dwellings data release (Published) Aotearoa Data Explorer*: population counts by ethnic group, age, gender; dwelling counts; iwi affiliation and Māori descent counts; totals by topic (to SA1) Aotearoa New Zealand as a village of 100 infographic Customised data requests October 2024 – Integrated Data Infrastructure (IDI) refresh (Published) Individuals and dwellings data 23 October 2024 – Electoral data release (Published) Number of electorates and electoral populations (CSV, web maps) 31 October 2024 – Te Ara Takatū (Published) Customised iwi data requests on families, extended families, and households data Metadata
3 October 2024 – Individuals and dwellings data release (Published)

Note: Releases may be supported by other products including information releases, news stories and maps. *Aotearoa Data Explorer includes: New Zealand totals Regional council areas Territorial authority and Auckland local board areas Statistical area 1 (SA1) Statistical area 2 (SA2) Statistical area 3 (SA3) – selected tables Urban-rural areas – selected tables Wards – selected tables Health New Zealand – Te Whatu Ora regions and districts
Note: Releases may be supported by other products including information releases, news stories and maps.
*Aotearoa Data Explorer includes:
- Regional council areas
- Territorial authority and Auckland local board areas
- Statistical area 1 (SA1)
- Statistical area 2 (SA2)
- Statistical area 3 (SA3) – selected tables
- Urban-rural areas – selected tables
- Wards – selected tables
- Health New Zealand – Te Whatu Ora regions and districts
Census | Stats NZ includes stories about how census data benefits communities.
For general enquiries and data requests please contact our Information Centre:
Stats NZ freephone : 0508 525 525 (NZ only). Email : info@stats.govt.nz .
Subscribe to our Census Advisory newsletter to receive regular updates about our work.
Stats NZ Sandi Reily 021 285 9191 census.communications@stats.govt.nz
Te Kāhui Raraunga Maia Renner comms@kahuiraraunga.io
